response.justification: Bogdan brings 17 years of broad software engineering depth and genuine staff-level credibility, and his current work on MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq is directly on-point for this role's key technologies. However, Python — the primary language listed — is absent from his skills entirely, his hands-on AI/GenAI experience is confined to his current role starting April 2025, and the resume contains no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. The delta between 17 years of general software craftsmanship and the 8+ years of production AI delivery the role requires is material and would need to be stress-tested in a screen.
strengths: Active MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and framework development in current role — direct alignment with a listed key technology that few candidates will have

Staff-level track record across data-intensive, high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s; Optilyz: multi-country pricing pipelines on AWS CDK) demonstrating production-grade engineering discipline
Strong public teaching and mentorship record — workshops, conference talks, and OSS tooling — matching the engineering-excellence and mentorship responsibilities of the role
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills list and the resume narrative, despite being the first-listed required language and dominant language for AI/ML tooling
Verifiable AI/GenAI experience begins only in April 2025; the role mandates 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions, leaving a roughly 7-year gap
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation or observability frameworks, or named multi-agent frameworks — all of which are explicit required or preferred qualifications
